model_findings.description: The `serve --openai` command accepts any `--host` value (default 127.0.0.1) without validation, warning, or enforcement of localhost-only binding. Because the FastAPI app has no authentication or rate limiting, binding to 0.0.0.0 exposes the subprocess backends (Codex, Claude, Gemini, Hermes) to the network.

ChatCompletionRequest.messages and Message.content accept unbounded lists and strings with no max_length or request-size limits in Pydantic or FastAPI. Large untrusted payloads are flattened via _flatten_messages and passed via stdin or --oneshot to long-running backends, creating an easy local (or misbound) denial-of-service vector.

The chat_completions handler declares model_config extra='allow' and therefore silently accepts stream=true (and other OpenAI parameters) but always executes a blocking non-streaming backend call and returns a completed JSON response, breaking clients that expect SSE streaming behavior.

Documentation states that the grok-4.3 route invokes `hermes -z PROMPT -m grok-4.3`, yet the actual _hermes_backend implementation uses `--oneshot={prompt}`; the mismatch will confuse anyone trying to reproduce or extend the proxy from the docs.
